title: amyema hilliana
intro: Amyema hilliana, also known as the Hill's Mistletoe, is a species of mistletoe that is native to Australia. It is a hemiparasitic plant, obtaining its nutrients by attaching itself to the branches of its host tree and siphoning off water and nutrients. Traditionally, the leaves of the Hill's Mistletoe have been used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic actions. The leaves are often prepared as a tea or tincture, and are said to offer a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function.
